seoTitle: Create an Invoice and Receive Payments in QuickBooks
metaDescription: How to build a customer invoice in QuickBooks Online and then record the matching payment so your accounts receivable stays accurate.
intro: Getting paid reliably starts with a clean invoice-to-payment workflow. QuickBooks Online lets you build a professional invoice, send it to a client, then log the payment once it comes in — keeping your open balances accurate the whole way through. This guide walks through both halves of that process.

steps.heading: Start a new invoice
steps.body: Click + New, then choose Invoice under the Customers column to open the creation form.

steps.heading: Select the customer and terms
steps.body: Choose an existing client or add a new one, then set your billing terms such as Net 30 or Due on Receipt.

steps.heading: Add line items
steps.body: In the product or service grid, select your items, then enter quantities and rates for each line.

steps.heading: Save and send
steps.body: Review the total, enable sales tax if applicable, then click Save and Send to deliver the invoice.

steps.heading: Record the payment
steps.body: When payment arrives, go to + New and select Receive Payment, choose the customer, check the specific invoice being settled, pick the deposit account, and save.

faq.question: What happens if I don't record the payment after it's received?
faq.answer: The invoice stays marked as open, which throws off your accounts receivable reporting even though the money has already come in.

faq.question: Can I apply one payment to multiple invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, the Receive Payment screen shows all open invoices for that customer, and you can check off more than one to apply the payment against.

faq.question: Where does the payment money go once I record it?
faq.answer: You choose between Undeposited Funds or a direct bank account in the Deposit To field, depending on how you handle your daily deposits.

faq.question: How do I check if an invoice is still unpaid?
faq.answer: Running an Accounts Receivable Aging Detail report shows you every open invoice and how long it's been outstanding.
